title: Craft vs Obsidian for Power users
meta_description: Craft vs Obsidian for power users who want local file control, extensibility, and long-term second brain flexibility.
constraint_lens: You want full control over your files and the freedom to extend your system without platform limits.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for power users building an extensible second brain.
one_second_verdict.reason: Craft fails first because block-level structure and export flexibility are constrained by a hosted model.

verdict.summary: Obsidian wins for power users building an extensible second brain. It stores notes as local Markdown files and supports a large community plugin ecosystem. Craft runs as a hosted workspace with block-based documents and export limitations tied to its model. If block-level structure and export flexibility are constrained by a hosted model, Craft fails first.
verdict.decision_rule: If block-level structure and export flexibility are constrained by a hosted model, Craft fails first.

sections.heading: Why Obsidian fits Power users better
sections.content: Obsidian fits this power user because the winning mechanism improves setup, daily retrieval, and long-term note structure rather than solving only one narrow problem.

bullets.point: Obsidian gives the user more control where the rule says it matters most
bullets.why_it_matters: The stronger mechanism keeps the note system from hitting the same limit over and over.

bullets.point: Obsidian improves daily note handling in a practical way
bullets.why_it_matters: The archive becomes easier to navigate and use instead of staying static.

bullets.point: Obsidian leaves more room for the workflow to expand
bullets.why_it_matters: That helps when the user is choosing for a system they expect to lean on heavily.

bullets.point: Craft keeps the workflow lighter in a narrower use case
bullets.why_it_matters: The losing tool can still win when the deeper mechanism is not doing much real work yet.

bullets.point: Craft favors direct writing over more system behavior
bullets.why_it_matters: That matters when the note job is simple and the richer setup would mostly stay idle.

bullets.point: Craft asks for less commitment up front
bullets.why_it_matters: The simpler surface can be better when the user wants notes to stay straightforward.

sections.heading: Where each tool can break down

items.fails_when: Obsidian becomes heavier than necessary when the deeper mechanism is not doing enough real work yet.
items.what_to_do_instead: Choose Craft if the simpler note model is still sufficient.

items.fails_when: Craft breaks down when the archive keeps hitting the exact limit named in the rule.
items.what_to_do_instead: Choose Obsidian once that deeper capability matters daily.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: This can flip if the deeper mechanism stops doing enough daily work to justify its added complexity. Then Craft may be the better tradeoff.

sections.heading: Quick decision rules
rules: Choose Obsidian when the deeper mechanism in the rule is already doing real work.
Choose Craft when the simpler alternative is still enough.
Avoid Craft when the same limit keeps repeating across daily note use.

faqs.q: Why does Obsidian fit the rule better?
faqs.a: Obsidian turns the mechanism in the decision rule into gains across setup, daily retrieval, and long-term note structure instead of only one narrow feature win.
Craft is still reasonable when the deeper capability is not yet doing enough work to justify itself.

faqs.q: What usually signals it is time to switch?
faqs.a: The same limit keeps appearing in more than one part of the note workflow.
No. It is about whether the deeper mechanism is now operationally useful.
